description:
The clavain-cli shim falls back to Bash mode when deployed via plugin cache because the Go binary cannot be built. Two root causes:

1. vendor-src/cxdb/clients/go/ is empty in published cache — the directory exists but contains no files.

2. intercore replace directive resolves outside plugin tree — go.mod has replace github.com/mistakeknot/intercore => ../../../../core/intercore which only resolves inside the Sylveste monorepo, not in the isolated plugin cache at ~/.claude/plugins/cache/interagency-marketplace/clavain/.

Build error from cache: intent.go:12:2 replacement directory ../../../../core/intercore does not exist; cxdb_client.go:13:2 replacement directory ../../vendor-src/cxdb/clients/go does not exist.

Build succeeds from source tree (os/Clavain/cmd/clavain-cli/).

Impact: 34 Go-only commands unavailable in Bash fallback, including sprint-init, record-cost-estimate, compose, intent, policy-check, daemon, all cxdb-* and scenario-* commands. The sprint skill workflow breaks at Step 1 (sprint-init).

Suggested fix: Pre-compile the Go binary during ic publish / interpub and include clavain-cli-go in the published artifact, rather than relying on auto-build from source in the cache.
title: clavain-cli Go binary fails to build in plugin cache — 34 commands unavailable

description: Apple's M5 Max has a 16-core ANE that sits idle during MLX GPU inference. This would break the serial GPU→SSD→GPU pipeline constraint that flash-moe identified. Risk: ANE programming is undocumented beyond CoreML. Approach: profile ANE utilization during inference, test CoreML attention dispatch.
title: [interfere] Experiment: ANE (Apple Neural Engine) offload for attention

description:
Research epic for making 700B-1T parameter MoE models run at usable interactive speeds (8+ tok/s) on M5 Max 128GB via SSD streaming.

## Context
flash-moe proved the approach works: Qwen3.5-397B at 11.1 tok/s via Metal + pread() + OS page cache. But flash-moe is hardcoded for one architecture. The challenge is generalizing this to the other frontier MoE models (Kimi K2.5 1T, GLM-5 744B, DeepSeek V3.2 672B) which are all too large for 128GB RAM (350-420GB quantized).

## Baseline (2026-03-27)
- Qwen3.5-397B-A17B 4-bit: 11.1 tok/s (flash-moe, cache-io-split 4)
- Expert I/O: 37.5ms/tok (41.7% of total) — the bottleneck
- M5 Max SSD: ~36 GB/s sequential read bandwidth
- OS page cache hit rate: ~71% (M3 Max), expected higher on 128GB

## Target
8+ tok/s decode on all four models (Qwen 397B, Kimi 1T, GLM-5 744B, DeepSeek V3.2 672B). This is the threshold where streaming text matches reading speed — usable for interactive chat.

## Approaches to investigate
1. **Generalized SSD streaming engine** — abstract flash-moe's Metal+pread pattern into a model-agnostic framework, or port per-model
2. **mlx-lm with mmap offloading** — simplest path, unknown perf (likely 1-3 tok/s without optimization)
3. **Page cache optimization** — madvise hints, expert pre-warming, memory wiring (tracked in sylveste-308, sylveste-xc8)
4. **Expert clustering/reordering** — co-locate frequently co-activated experts for sequential SSD reads
5. **Hybrid approach** — keep attention + routing in GPU memory, stream only expert FFN from SSD
6. **llama.cpp partial offload** — if GGUF conversions exist

## Success criteria
- All 4 models running at 8+ tok/s on M5 Max 128GB
- Coherent output quality (not degraded by streaming)
- Documented approach reproducible by others

## Models (all downloaded)
- Qwen3.5-397B-A17B 4-bit: ~/Models/mlx-community-Qwen3.5-397B-A17B-4bit/ (209GB)
- Kimi K2.5 3-bit: ~/.cache/huggingface/models/Kimi-K2.5-3bit/ (418GB)
- GLM-5 4-bit: ~/.cache/huggingface/models/GLM-5-4bit/ (390GB)
- DeepSeek V3.2 4-bit: ~/.cache/huggingface/models/DeepSeek-V3.2-4bit/ (352GB)
title: [interfere] Epic: SSD streaming inference for 700B+ MoE models — target 8+ tok/s

description: Implement attention sink pattern (Xiao et al. 2023, arXiv 2309.17453) for long-running agent sessions. Keep KV states for initial tokens (attention sinks) + recent window only. Enables stable generation over 4M+ tokens without growing KV cache. 22x speedup over sliding window recompute. Critical for Clavain agents that run long sessions — currently context is bounded by KV cache size. mlx-lm already has --max-kv-size for a rotating cache; this extends it with attention sink preservation.
title: [interfere] Experiment: StreamingLLM attention sinks for infinite context

description:
Interlab autoresearch campaign to optimize page cache hit rate for SSD-streamed MoE inference.

**Primary metric**: cache_hit_rate_pct (higher_is_better)
**Secondary metric**: tok_s on 397B model
**Benchmark command**: `bash interlab-pagecache-tune.sh`
**Files in scope**: `server/ssd_streaming.py`, `server/expert_prefetch.py`
**Mutation space**:
  - madvise hint strategy: MADV_WILLNEED vs MADV_SEQUENTIAL vs MADV_RANDOM
  - Pre-warm depth: 0 (none), 1 (next predicted expert), 2 (next 2), 3
  - Prediction method: last-token routing scores vs moving average vs markov chain
  - Memory wiring threshold: wire top-N most-used experts (N=0..16)
  - Page cache reservation: 0GB (OS default), 16GB, 32GB, 48GB
  - Expert access reordering: original order vs frequency-sorted vs clustering
**Kill condition**: 10 experiments with <1% cache hit improvement
**Prerequisite**: flash-moe baseline benchmark (sylveste-4l2) + 397B download

Baseline from flash-moe investigation:
  - M5 Max SSD: 36 GB/s sequential, 27 GB/s random pread
  - Expected ~71% cache hit rate without optimization (flash-moe M3 baseline)
  - Target: 85%+ cache hit rate with smart pre-fetching

description: Google's TurboQuant (ICLR 2026, arxiv.org/abs/2504.19874) achieves 3-bit KV cache quantization with zero accuracy loss on long-context benchmarks. Two-stage approach: (1) PolarQuant converts KV to polar coordinates, maps to fixed circular grid (eliminates expensive normalization), (2) QJL adds 1-bit residual error correction via Johnson-Lindenstrauss Transform. Results: 6x KV memory reduction, 8x performance increase on H100 GPUs vs 32-bit. Tested on Gemma, Mistral, Llama-3.1-8B. This is strictly better than our planned Q8K/Q4V approach (sylveste-dw8) — 3-bit with ZERO quality loss vs 4-8 bit with potential degradation. For interfere: (1) implement PolarQuant for MLX/Metal, (2) measure KV savings on Qwen3.5-35B and 122B, (3) combine with StreamingLLM sinks for near-infinite context. Companion paper: PolarQuant (AISTATS 2026, arxiv.org/abs/2502.02617).
title: [interfere] Experiment: TurboQuant (PolarQuant+QJL) for 3-bit KV cache compression

description:
Interlab autoresearch campaign to optimize concurrent request batching.

**Primary metric**: aggregate_tok_s (higher_is_better, sum across N clients)
**Quality gate**: P99 latency ≤ 2x single-request latency
**Benchmark command**: `bash interlab-batching-tune.sh`
**Files in scope**: `server/batch_scheduler.py`, `server/metal_worker.py`
**Mutation space**:
  - Batch accumulation timeout: 5ms–100ms
  - Max batch size: 2–8 sequences
  - Priority weight for high-pri requests: 1.0–10.0
  - Preemption threshold: never / after-prefill / after-N-tokens
  - Sequence padding strategy: left-pad vs right-pad vs no-pad
**Kill condition**: 10 experiments with no throughput gain vs sequential
**Prerequisite**: Batch scheduler implementation (sylveste-5ne)

Context: MLX has no concurrent inference (ml-explore/mlx#3078).
Batching works within the sequential constraint by sharing prefill computation.
Common case: 3-5 concurrent Clavain subagents during flux-drive reviews.
title: [interfere] Autoresearch: adaptive batching parameters for concurrent agents

description: When multiple Clavain agents send requests simultaneously, currently they queue sequentially (MLX constraint: no concurrent inference). Research: (1) continuous batching — accumulate requests into micro-batches for shared prefill, (2) sequence-level parallelism within a batch, (3) request priority with preemption (interrupt low-pri generation when high-pri arrives). Expected: better throughput for the common case of 3-5 concurrent subagents during flux-drive reviews.
title: [interfere] Experiment: adaptive batching for concurrent agent requests

The Zollman effect: sparse networks find truth more reliably because they preserve independent reasoning longer. Source: fd-swarm-ensemble-frontier.
title: Sparse communication topology (small-world > fully-connected)

description: Seven empirically-grounded rules from collective intelligence research: (1) Blind-estimate-then-reveal (Delphi), (2) Briefing + small-pod deliberation + question generation (Fishkin), (3) Enforce diversity + independence before aggregation (Surowiecki), (4) Conductor agent manages flow not content (salon culture), (5) Authentic dissent not theatrical devil's advocacy (Nemeth), (6) Parallel drafts with retrospective selection (Dennett/Minsky), (7) Conditional question decomposition (combinatorial markets). Meta-finding: collective intelligence is a property of discourse structure, not aggregation methods. Source: fd-deliberative-emergence.
title: Seven deliberative orchestration rules (Delphi, markets, assemblies)

description: Five formal turn-taking protocols from jazz/Socratic/improv that compose across layers: (1) Pressing Cycle — referent-guided generate-evaluate with novelty/coherence band (d_min < d < d_max), (2) Morris Conduction Protocol — 7 typed structural signals (sustain/develop/superimpose/literal/enter/exit/cut), (3) Lorenzen Dialogue Game — formal attack/defense grammar preventing sycophancy, (4) Yes-And with Degeneration Guards — density/drift limits + reincorporation, (5) Sawyer Flow Envelope — participation Gini < 0.3, novelty_rate > epsilon, response_relevance > 0.7. Source: fd-jazz-socratic-improv.
title: Five composable discourse protocols (Pressing/Conduction/Lorenzen/Yes-And/Sawyer)

description: Remove 'geniuses' from all positioning language. Replace with 'cognitively diverse' and 'interdisciplinary' — these map to real architecture (interflux cognitive lenses) and are not claimable by competitors without the mechanism. 'Geniuses' is an unverifiable superlative that fails falsifiability, competitor absorption, and developer credibility tests. Source: fd-metaphor-market, fd-salon-epistemics.
title: Drop 'geniuses' from positioning — use 'cognitively diverse'

description: From termite mound construction: agents write to a shared CRDT document, the document IS the work product AND the coordination medium. No orchestrator routes messages. Each contribution changes document state, which changes what other agents perceive and respond to. Eliminates N-squared communication. Scales with document complexity, not agent count. Key design choice: agents never address each other, only the document. Source: fd-stigmergic-discourse (Macrotermes, CodeCRDT 2025).
title: Sematectonic substrate — document IS the coordination signal

description: Four pheromone channels on CRDT contributions: salience (importance), contention (disagreement), novelty (new concept), evidence (grounded support). Each decays exponentially unless reinforced by engagement. Maps to intermem's existing decay model. Contributions nobody engages with fade below visibility threshold. Contributions sustaining high salience promote from C5 ephemeral to C4 curated. Source: fd-stigmergic-discourse.
title: Pheromone gradient fields — multi-channel relevance with decay

description: From slime mould: discourse starts with maximal connectivity, then tracks information flow. High-flow paths (cited, built-upon) thicken; low-flow paths (read but ignored) thin. Self-organizing from dense mesh to efficient reasoning tree. Differs from time-based decay — old contributions that are still cited stay prominent. Needs graph infrastructure. Source: fd-stigmergic-discourse (Physarum polycephalum, Tero et al. Science 2010).
title: Physarum topology optimization — prune reasoning graphs by flow

description: From ant nest architecture: two coordination layers — stigmergic amplification (emergent, bottom-up) + template constraints (top-down structural requirements). E.g., 'A Synthesis cannot be written until at least 3 independent critiques exist.' 'No more than 2 contributions from same agent in same subgraph.' Maps to Sylveste's existing phase-gate architecture applied to discourse structure. Source: fd-stigmergic-discourse.
title: Template boundaries — structural gates on discourse

description: From perfumery: assign agents to volatility tiers. Top-note agents (fast, reactive, sacrificial provocations). Heart agents (medium, core analysis, 2-3 turns to develop). Base agents (slow, persistent context, always-on). ALL tiers active from start — progressive revelation, not sequential relay. The emergent quality: directed depth with temporal evolution. Source: fd-perfumery-accord.
title: Volatility-stratified ensemble (top/heart/base agent tiers)

description: From perfumery drydown: Opening phase (turns 1-3, divergent, many ideas). Heart phase (turns 4-10, primary analysis, fast agents reduce output). Drydown phase (turns 10+, integrative depth, base agents prominent). Key: drydown quality must be designed at composition time, not at transition time. Base agents configured at start; their contribution is revealed, not added, as volatile agents evaporate. Source: fd-perfumery-accord.
title: Drydown pattern — designed temporal phases in discourse

description: Pre-configure discourse modes: Chypre (contrasting synthesis — critic+advocate+integrator), Fougere (layered expertise — overlapping specialists), Oriental (deep convergence — sequential building), Fresh/Citrus (rapid divergence — many independent takes). Each is a proven configuration in accord-space. The orchestrator monitors for drift out of the chosen family's basin. Source: fd-perfumery-accord.
title: Fragrance families as discourse archetypes

description: From perfumery: some behaviors have sharp non-linear thresholds. Contrarianism at 20% improves quality; at 80% makes convergence impossible. Abstraction at 10% provides perspective; at 50% prevents concrete progress. Monitor contribution ratios per behavioral mode. Detect cliff approach. Build redundancy: multiple agents performing critical functions (like the robust rose accord). Source: fd-perfumery-accord.
title: Indole cliff — ratio governance for agent behaviors

description: From Bay of Fundy tidal resonance: when prompt injection cadence matches the round-trip latency of the agent topology, constructive interference amplifies signal quality for free. Measure empirical round-trip latency, set orchestrator cadence to match. Tune ±10-20% to find resonant frequency empirically. Source: fd-tidal-resonance.
title: Quarter-wavelength resonance — match cadence to topology's natural period

description: From AMOC: two storage tiers — ephemeral fast-exchange bus (surface) + persistent knowledge store with slow update cycles (deep). Define 'downwelling' criteria: insight enters deep layer when N agents endorse or survives K adversarial rounds. Define 'upwelling' triggers: new topic queries deep layer for relevant priors. Deep layer has transit time — insights propagate slowly, becoming available at different times. Source: fd-tidal-resonance.
title: Thermohaline dual-timescale relay — fast/slow agent coupling

description: Every factual claim entering multi-agent discourse must carry provenance: {source: direct_generation|retrieved_document|agent_relay, origin_agent, generation_step, retrievable}. Claims tagged 'agent_relay' are inadmissible unless the originating agent is re-queried or the claim falls within recognized exceptions (tool output, structured retrieval with logged queries). Blocks hallucination propagation through citation chains — the most common failure mode in multi-agent pipelines. Source: fd-judicial-evidence (hearsay rule, FRE 801-802), fd-monastic-scriptoria (stemma codicum).
title: Implement hearsay rule — provenance metadata on agent citations

description: From estuarine mixing: the interface between epistemically distinct agent populations is the most productive zone. Design agent populations with deliberately different properties, connect through shared interface channel. Monitor for the 'null zone' where neither dominates. If one population overwhelms (river flood destroying salt wedge), the productive boundary collapses. Source: fd-tidal-resonance.
title: Estuarine halocline — productive boundary zones between agent populations

description: From coastal Kelvin waves: information propagates coherently along boundaries between agent clusters, not through bulk broadcast. Dedicated boundary agents participate in 2+ clusters. Unidirectional flow prevents information loops. Exponential decay away from boundary prevents overload. Source: fd-tidal-resonance.
title: Kelvin wave boundary propagation — route info along cluster edges

description: From AMOC collapse precursors: monitor three sliding-window metrics — (a) variance of per-round quality scores, (b) autocorrelation of agent outputs (are agents echoing prior round?), (c) novelty ratio (fraction of new content per round). When all three move in warning direction simultaneously, inject disruption (new agent, new topic, adversarial challenge). Source: fd-tidal-resonance.
title: AMOC tipping monitor — early warning for discourse collapse

description: From common law: layer multiple independent error-correction mechanisms. (1) Provenance attestation (hearsay rule), (2) Original source requirement (best evidence), (3) Domain competence gate (Daubert/voir dire), (4) Assertion burden framework (burden of proof), (5) Adversarial triad (confrontation), (6) Foundation check protocol (conditional relevance), (7) Error review with harmless/reversible classification (appellate). No single detector suffices; the stack catches what each layer misses. Source: fd-judicial-evidence.
title: Legal protocol stack — 7-layer error correction for agent discourse

description: From burden of proof doctrine: for claims that are verifiable but unverified, the system presumes confabulation until grounded. Inverts the natural LLM tendency to treat fluent generation as evidence of truth. Graduated standards: low stakes (preponderance), medium stakes (clear and convincing, multiple evidence lines), high stakes (beyond reasonable doubt, tool-grounded verification). Source: fd-judicial-evidence.
title: Presumption of hallucination — invert the default

description: From textual criticism: tag every agent output with a provenance vector. When hallucination detected downstream, trace backward through provenance graph. Shared hallucinations across multiple branches identify the 'hyparchetype' (introducing turn). Uses computational phylogenetics — shared errors identify shared ancestry. Build the stemma from error patterns, not content review. Source: fd-monastic-scriptoria.
title: Stemma-based hallucination tracing via provenance vectors

description: From medieval corrector role: dedicated agent whose only job is annotation and error flagging — never content generation. Different system prompt, different eval criteria, ideally different model/temperature. Authority from positional role, not content expertise. Compares outputs against source material (systematic correction) or applies heuristic consistency checks (casual correction). Never asked to 'fix' — only to mark discrepancies. Source: fd-monastic-scriptoria.
title: Correctorius — structurally separated annotation-only agent

description: From university pecia system: maintain canonical exemplar (shared context doc, validated knowledge base) that agents read from but never write to directly. Each agent's output has independent errors. Changes to shared knowledge go through approval gate before entering exemplar. Prevents hallucination cascade — no agent reads another's unchecked output as ground truth. Source: fd-monastic-scriptoria.
title: Pecia system — exemplar-controlled parallel production

description: From monastic reading practice: four passes at increasing abstraction on the SAME source material. (1) Literal extraction — surface facts, (2) Analytical decomposition — meaning and implications, (3) Adversarial engagement — questions and challenges, (4) Essence distillation — minimum decision-relevant representation. Each pass references the ORIGINAL input, not prior pass output. Prevents telephone-game distortion. Source: fd-monastic-scriptoria.
title: Lectio divina — four-stage deepening on same source

description: From Aboriginal songlines: the conversation IS the garden. Discourse structure should mirror knowledge structure — sequence encodes conceptual dependency. An agent re-entering should 'walk the discourse' sequentially and reconstruct the knowledge. Argues against arbitrary turn-taking and toward deliberate discourse topology where conversation order mirrors conceptual dependency. Source: fd-indigenous-wayfinding.
title: Songline isomorphism — discourse structure IS knowledge structure

description: Monitor per-round disagreement rate between agents during multi-round discourse. When disagreement rate drops faster than expected (agents capitulating), inject corrective prompts: 'You previously disagreed with X on point Y. Articulate your strongest remaining objection before conceding.' Based on CONSENSAGENT (ACL 2025) — runtime sycophancy detection via disagreement rate monitoring. The peer-findings JSONL timeline could serve as the monitoring substrate. Source: fd-swarm-ensemble-frontier (CONSENSAGENT), fd-deliberative-emergence (Nemeth authentic dissent research).
title: Add sycophancy monitor — track disagreement rate decay

description: From Polynesian navigation: encode same question across genuinely different epistemic approaches (formal analysis, empirical evidence, analogical reasoning, adversarial stress-testing). Agreement across modalities = high confidence. Disagreement = the most important signal ('swells don't match stars, therefore unaccounted current'). Never designate one agent as canonical truth-source. Track convergence and flag divergence. Source: fd-indigenous-wayfinding.
title: Multi-modal redundancy without canonical source

description: From Micronesian etak: instead of one canonical 'state of discussion' document, let each agent maintain its own reference frame. Track angular relationships between agent perspectives rather than absolute positions. 'I'm closer to Agent B's position than C's' is more robust than any absolute state summary. Radical but no clear implementation path. Source: fd-indigenous-wayfinding.
title: Etak reference frame inversion — relative positioning over canonical state

description: From Polynesian navigation: multi-agent discussions accumulate drift (misunderstandings, assumption shifts, terminology slippage). Track 'epistemic elapsed time' — how long since discourse grounded against verifiable external reference. Trigger recalibration when confidence should have degraded below threshold. High-ambiguity topics degrade faster. Reinitialize from landmark when divergence detected. Source: fd-indigenous-wayfinding.
title: Dead reckoning with landmark recalibration — epistemic elapsed time

description: From Polynesian navigation community: completeness is a property of the collective, not any individual. Each agent is a genuine specialist with deep domain knowledge, plus enough boundary knowledge to validate adjacent agents' contributions. The 'pwo protocol' — structured initiation determining what knowledge each agent accesses. Explicitly NOT the 'committee of generalists' pattern. Source: fd-indigenous-wayfinding.
title: Distributed apprenticeship — overlapping specialists, not generalist committees

description: Design gap surfaced by the salon metaphor: no agent is explicitly tasked with contrarian provocation. Different from devil's advocacy (which Nemeth shows reinforces initial views). The provocateur has a genuinely different analytical framework, not a theatrical opposition role. Should introduce uncomfortable reframings, not just object. Source: fd-design-affordances, fd-deliberative-emergence (Nemeth 2001).
title: Provocateur agent — explicit contrarian role for groupthink prevention

description: A garden has seasons. Run themed review cycles where all reviews emphasize a particular dimension for a defined period (security month, performance quarter). During a 'security season,' fd-safety gets elevated weight, security-adjacent agents are invited to reviews they'd normally be triaged out of. At season's end, evaluate whether emphasis improved outcomes. Fights Goodhart pressure by preventing any single metric from dominating indefinitely. Source: fd-design-affordances.
title: Salon Seasons — rotating thematic emphasis cycles

description: With configurable probability, include an agent from an unrelated domain in reviews. fd-game-design reviews infrastructure code. fd-perception reviews API design. Track whether these 'cross-pollination' dispatches produce disproportionately valuable findings (measured by human action rate). If evidence supports, increase probability. If not, prune. The salon's value comes from unexpected intellectual encounters. Source: fd-design-affordances.
title: Cross-pollination dispatch — deliberate expertise mismatch

description: From monastic practice: some knowledge SHOULD be ephemeral and locally mutable (working hypotheses, intermediate reasoning). Some should be frozen and version-controlled (validated facts, canonical documents). Not all agent memory should persist. Oral = living, embodied, personal, allowed to drift. Written = fixed, transmissible, correctable. The trigger for writing down customs was the need to prevent local drift — apply same criterion to agent memory promotion. Source: fd-monastic-scriptoria.
title: Oral/written bifurcation — ephemeral vs persistent agent memory

description: From medieval quire signatures and catchwords: at every agent pipeline handoff, include lightweight semantic checksum. 'This block contains error analysis for module X' / 'I expect error analysis for module X.' If mismatch, halt and investigate. Catches dropped context, topic drift, and structural misalignment without full re-verification. Trivially cheap check that catches catastrophic assembly errors. Source: fd-monastic-scriptoria.
title: Catchwords — semantic checksums at handoff boundaries

description:
Compose existing subsystems (Mycroft fleet dispatch, Discovery pipeline, Thematic Lanes, Interspect learning loop, Self-dispatch, Skaffen) into the end-to-end autonomous epic flow:

Human provides theme/inspiration → system autonomously:
1. RESEARCH: Discovery pipeline + autoresearch scan the problem space
2. DESIGN: Auto-brainstorm → strategy → plan (currently human runs /clavain:sprint)
3. DECOMPOSE: Auto-create epic with child beads from plan (currently /clavain:write-plan)
4. EXECUTE: Mycroft dispatches sprints, self-dispatch claims beads, agents build+review
5. SHIP: Auto-merge when confidence thresholds met (L3/L4 autonomy)
6. COMPOUND: Interspect feeds outcomes back into routing for next epic

Human validates at strategic checkpoints only (epic approval, ship policy, exception escalation).

GAP ANALYSIS — what exists vs what's missing:
- EXISTS: Discovery pipeline (E5), Portfolio orchestration (E8), Self-dispatch loop, Mycroft brainstorm+plan, AI Factory PRD (3-wave), Interspect Phase 1+2 partial, Autonomy ladder L0-L2, Thematic lanes brainstorm
- MISSING: Research→Design autonomy (who turns a discovery signal into a brainstorm/strategy/plan?), Epic decomposition (who creates beads from a high-level goal?), Cross-sprint narrative continuity (who maintains epic-level coherence across 10 sprints?), Strategic validation gates (when does human check in — after sprint? after epic? only on ship?)

This is the L3 delegation level applied to multi-sprint work: 'Human sets theme and success criteria. System researches, designs, decomposes, executes, ships. Human validates epic outcome, not individual sprints.'

Depends on: Mycroft fleet orchestrator, Interspect Phase 2, self-dispatch loop, discovery pipeline.
References: docs/prds/2026-03-20-ai-factory-orchestration.md, docs/brainstorms/2026-03-12-mycroft-fleet-orchestrator-brainstorm.md, docs/plans/2026-03-20-self-dispatch-loop.md, docs/brainstorms/2026-02-21-thematic-work-lanes-brainstorm.md, PHILOSOPHY.md (delegation ladder L3), sylveste-vision.md (autonomy ladder L3-L4)
title: Autonomous Epic Execution — research→design→decompose→execute→ship with human policy governance

### 100-token runs
| Config | Median tok/s | Mean | P5/P95 | TTFT |

| Metric | 35B (3B active) | 122B (10B active) |
|--------|-----------------|-------------------|
| Median tok/s (100t) | 85.8 | 49.9 |
| TTFT | 0.155s | 0.225s |
| RAM footprint | ~19GB | ~65GB |

### Key findings:
1. **50 tok/s is usable** for real-time generation — comparable to cloud latency
2. kv_bits=8 has slight penalty on 122B (~8%) vs free lunch on 35B — larger model = more KV computation
3. kv_bits=4 has significant penalty on 122B (~34%) — not recommended at short context
4. 500-token runs hit thermal throttling hard (nominal→heavy, 50→25 tok/s)
5. 122B quality visibly better than 35B on reasoning tasks

description:
Interlab autoresearch campaign to optimize LayerSkip early exit thresholds.

**Primary metric**: tok/s (higher_is_better)
**Quality gate**: output match rate vs full-model baseline ≥ 95%
**Benchmark command**: `bash interlab-layerskip-tune.sh`
**Files in scope**: `server/experiments/early_exit.py`, `server/experiments/layerskip.py`
**Mutation space**:
  - Exit confidence threshold per layer group (early/mid/late): 0.80–0.99
  - Minimum layers to always run: 4–16
  - Confidence averaging window: 1–5 tokens
  - Layer group boundaries (which layers are "early" vs "late")
**Kill condition**: 10 consecutive experiments with <1% improvement
**Prerequisite**: LayerSkip implementation must exist first (sylveste-qbv)

Calibration data (from confidence profiler):
  - 122B: 67.5% tokens >99% confidence, 78.7% >90%
  - 35B: 64.4% tokens >99% confidence, 76.1% >90%
  - Theoretical max: ~1.5x speedup if 50% layers skipped for >99% tokens
title: [interfere] Autoresearch: LayerSkip exit threshold tuning

description:
Implement TurboQuant properly with custom Lloyd-Max centroids optimized for the post-rotation Beta distribution, plus a custom Metal attention kernel that uses non-uniform dequantization. This is the full paper-faithful implementation that v1 (polar) and v2 (rotation + affine) proved cannot be done with MLX's native quantization alone.

Key components:
1. Lloyd-Max 1D quantizer: precompute optimal centroids for Beta(d/2, d/2) distribution at each bit width (2,3,4,8). Store as lookup table.
2. Custom quantize/dequantize: per-coordinate centroid lookup instead of uniform grid + scale/bias.
3. Custom attention kernel: replaces mx.quantized_matmul with centroid-aware dequantize-then-matmul (or fused Metal kernel if performance requires).
4. Random orthogonal rotation matrix (QR decomposition) applied to K before quantization, Q at attention time.
5. Optional QJL residual correction for sub-3-bit.

Prerequisites: v2 experiment proved rotation works mathematically (fp16 path identical) but MLX affine quant negates the benefit. This bead is the proper implementation.

Kill condition: if custom Metal kernel is >2x slower than native mx.quantized_matmul at same bit width, abandon.
notes: Phase 1-2 COMPLETE (2026-03-27): Lloyd-Max solver, BHQ quantize/dequantize, BHQCacheWrapper with norm preservation, non-fused attention, benchmark CLI, 14 tests passing. Results: 2-bit BHQ 36% better than native, 3-bit 12% better, 4-bit native wins by 14%. Speed parity (no fused kernel needed). End-to-end generation on 0.5B-4bit model degrades — needs larger model validation. Phase 3 (fused Metal kernel) NOT needed per kill condition.
title: [interfere] TurboQuant v3: custom Lloyd-Max quantizer + attention kernel for MLX

| Config | Median tok/s | vs Baseline |
|--------|-------------|-------------|
| 122B alone | 49.9 | — |
| +0.8B draft (2 tok/step) | 36.4 | -27% (thermal confound) |
| +0.8B draft (3 tok/step) | 46.9 | **-6%** |
| +0.8B draft (5 tok/step) | 40.8 | -18% |

### 35B draft → 122B target (100 tokens)
| +35B draft (3 tok/step) | 14.9 | **-70%** |
| +35B draft (5 tok/step) | 11.4 | -77% |

### Key findings:
1. **Speculative decoding does not help on M5 Max** — all configurations slower than baseline
2. **Root cause: memory bandwidth bottleneck** — Apple Silicon unified memory means spec dec trades compute savings (which we don't need) for extra memory traffic (which is our bottleneck)
3. **Dense→MoE mismatch hurts acceptance rate** — 0.8B dense draft generates different tokens from MoE target
4. **Larger draft = more waste** — 35B draft is catastrophically slow
5. **Optimal if using at all**: 0.8B draft with 3 tokens/step (only -6% on 122B)
6. **Self-speculative (LayerSkip) is the right path** for Apple Silicon — no extra model, no extra memory traffic

description: Quantize the Key-Value cache to reduce memory pressure. Keys to 8-bit, Values to 4-bit (asymmetric quantization since values are more tolerant). KIVI (2024) shows 2-bit KV is feasible. ThinK (2024) shows channel pruning in KV cache gives 20% reduction composable with quantization. On 128GB M5 Max this could: (1) fit larger KV context windows, (2) leave more RAM for page cache when SSD-streaming experts, (3) enable running 397B with larger batch sizes. notes:
## KV Cache Quantization Benchmark Results (Qwen3.5-35B-A3B-4bit, M5 Max 128GB)

### 100-token runs (warm cache)
| Config | Median tok/s | Mean | P5/P95 | TTFT |
|--------|-------------|------|--------|------|
| none (fp16) | 85.8 | 80.3 | 43.1/110.4 | 0.155s |
| kv_bits=8 | 86.0 | 81.7 | 44.1/111.4 | 0.155s |
| kv_bits=4 | 81.6 | 75.8 | 35.8/103.9 | 0.171s |
| kv_bits=2 | 79.7 | 78.3 | 42.1/106.4 | 0.160s |

### 500-token runs (thermal throttled)
| Config | Median tok/s | Mean | P5/P95 | TTFT |
|--------|-------------|------|--------|------|
| none (fp16) | 53.8 | 67.1 | 27.5/122.5 | 0.218s |
| kv_bits=8 | 57.9 | 65.4 | 26.1/112.7 | 0.211s |
| kv_bits=4 | 53.3 | 59.0 | 25.1/110.1 | 0.247s |
| kv_bits=2 | 49.8 | 54.7 | 25.9/98.9 | 0.236s |

### Key findings:
1. **Quality: zero degradation at all levels** — factual, reasoning, and instruction outputs identical through kv_bits=2
2. **kv_bits=8: free lunch** — no throughput penalty, 2x KV memory reduction
3. **kv_bits=4: ~5% throughput cost** — 4x KV memory reduction, worth it for larger models
4. **kv_bits=2: ~7% throughput cost** — 8x KV memory reduction, quality still holds at 500 tokens
5. Thermal throttling dominates at 500-token runs (heavy thermal state), making speed differences noisy
6. The real win is memory headroom for 122B/397B models, not speed on the 35B

### Recommendation: default to kv_bits=8 for all models, use kv_bits=4 for 122B+

close_reason: NEGATIVE RESULT: Polar coordinate KV cache quantization does NOT work. Three iterations: (1) Original wrapper called inverse_polar_transform on quantized tuples — structurally broken. (2) Fixed dequantize-then-inverse with interleaved layout — 3.1x worse error. (3) Contiguous r/theta layout — 1.19x worse at 4-bit, still garbage output at ALL bit widths (2,4,8). Root cause: inverse trig transform amplifies quantization noise by 2.7x — angle error delta_theta becomes Cartesian error r*sin(delta_theta), proportional to radius. High-radius vectors (most important for attention) suffer most. This is a fundamental mathematical limitation. Kept code for reference.

description: Implement early exit with self-speculative verification (LayerSkip, arXiv 2404.16710). Early layers generate draft tokens, remaining layers verify. No separate draft model needed — uses the target model's own early layers. Expected: 1.8-2.2x speedup. notes:
## Confidence Profile Results (Early Exit Calibration)

### Tokens above confidence thresholds
| Model | >80% | >90% | >95% | >99% |
|-------|------|------|------|------|
| 0.8B dense | 71.4% | 64.2% | 56.8% | 44.8% |
| 35B MoE (3B active) | 81.7% | 76.1% | 71.0% | 64.4% |
| 122B MoE (10B active) | 84.1% | 78.7% | 74.0% | 67.5% |

### Key findings:
1. **Larger models are MORE confident** — higher fraction of easy tokens
2. **67.5% of 122B tokens have >99% confidence** — huge LayerSkip potential
3. **Creative writing has lowest confidence** (41-60% >99%) — layer skipping should be conservative here
4. **Coding and instruction following are most confident** (67-84% >99%) — best candidates for early exit
5. **MoE architecture concentrates confidence** — expert routing already "simplifies" many tokens

### Theoretical speedup if LayerSkip could skip 50% of layers for >99% tokens:
- 122B: 67.5% × 0.5 = 33.75% compute saved → ~1.5x speedup (50→75 tok/s)
- 35B: 64.4% × 0.5 = 32.2% compute saved → ~1.5x speedup (86→129 tok/s)
